game_name: Anno 1602: Creation of a New World

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player must find and colonize a nearby island immediately
Build economy through production chains and trade
Satisfying basic needs unlocks further buildings
Demand more sophisticated goods like cloth and alcohol
Require tobacco and higher-tier economic structures
Demand spices and most sophisticated goods; unlock endgame possibilities
Buildings must be upgraded to satisfy citizens and generate tax income
Unlocked after reaching population threshold; require dedicated factories and ongoing maintenance costs
Available for defense or conquest; can be upgraded for cargo capacity
Discover chains of islands to expand trade network
Trade with own colonies, neutral CPU factions, native tribes, and free traders
Can engage in trade or military conflict with up to 4 other human players

typical_relief_triggers: Successful population advancement to new social class
typical_tension_triggers: Tax income loss from unsatisfied citizens
Expensive building construction and maintenance costs
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive difficulty curve with mechanics introduced gradually; game pace responds dynamically to player action speed through progressive AI system

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Player discovers archipelago chains at own pace; free exploration structure with optional scenarios

difficulty_curve.early_game: Safe colonization phase: find island, establish settlement, basic economy. Introduction to population class system with Pioneers.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate complexity: manage multiple population classes (Settlers, Citizens), introduce technology upgrades, island discovery, and trade network expansion. Optional diplomacy with neutral factions.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex economy management: Merchants class demands, advanced technology upgrades, military production chains unlocked. Optional military conquest or economic victory through trade dominance.
notable_spikes: First military unit unlock (requires factories and constant maintenance costs)
Transition to Merchant class (highest demands and most sophisticated production chains)

notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign scenarios introduce new mechanics over time through structured progression rather than all at once
notable_level_patterns.example_level: 6 scenarios in EU release, 15 total in NA release

notable_level_patterns.description: Reaching population thresholds unlocks new building types and production possibilities, creating cascading gameplay progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Weapon factory access requires specific population level

notable_level_patterns.description: Island discovery chains encourage organic exploration and expansion without linear level gating
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Free-play mode with random map generation

notable_level_patterns.description: Economic dominance path vs. military conquest path allows different playstyle progressions through same scenario
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any scenario can be completed peacefully or militarily
